message:
fix(newproj): harden directory checks, fix bash arithmetic, update br CLI syntax

- screen_directory: check read/write/execute permissions on existing dirs
  before accepting them; use find -maxdepth 1 instead of ls -A for
  emptiness check; normalize path on warning-accepted dirs; handle
  read failure from /dev/tty as back-navigation; support "<back" command
  in fallback mode
- screen_project_name: handle read failure from /dev/tty as back-navigation;
  support "<back" command in fallback mode; capitalize prompt label
- screen_tech_stack: auto-detect tech stack from collected project_dir
  instead of $PWD (avoids misclassifying new projects when the wizard runs
  from an unrelated repo); accept "<back" as back navigation; fix
  10#num -> 10#$num bash arithmetic to prevent octal parse errors
- screen_features: fix 10#num -> 10#$num arithmetic
- screen_success: update br create examples to use --title/--type flags
- newproj.sh: update br create examples to --title/--type flag syntax;
  init logging when ACFS_SESSION_LOG is unset in interactive mode
- newproj_agents.sh: update br create example to --title/--type syntax
- newproj_tui.sh: fix 10#num -> 10#$num in read_selection() and
  read_checkbox(); quote array length in comparisons
- Update expect tests (happy_path, error_handling, navigation) to match
  renamed screen titles: "Welcome to ACFS Project Setup", "Choose a
  Project Name", "Choose Project Location", "Select Tech Stack",
  "Select Project Features", "Creating Project...", "Project Created!",
  "PROJECT CREATED SUCCESSFULLY"; change send_back to use escape key
- Add unit test for directory permission rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -326,8 +326,8 @@ br ready --json
 Create issues:
 
 ```bash
-br create "Issue title" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
-br create "Issue title" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:br-123 --json
+br create --title="Issue title" --type=bug --priority=1 --json
+br create --title="Issue title" --type=task --priority=1 --deps discovered-from:br-123 --json
 ```
 
 Update:
@@ -498,6 +498,10 @@ run_interactive_mode() {
     # Source the screens module
     source "$NEWPROJ_SCRIPT_DIR/newproj_screens.sh"
 
+    if [[ -z "${ACFS_SESSION_LOG:-}" ]]; then
+        init_logging
+    fi
+
     # Pre-fill state if arguments provided
     if [[ -n "$prefill_name" ]]; then
         echo -e "${CYAN}Pre-filling project name: $prefill_name${NC}"

@@ -486,7 +486,7 @@ br ready
 
 Create issues:
 ```bash
-br create "Issue title" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4
+br create --title="Issue title" --type=bug --priority=1
 ```
 
 Update:

@@ -92,18 +92,34 @@ check_directory_status() {
 
     # Check if exists
     if [[ -e "$resolved" ]]; then
-        if [[ -d "$resolved" ]]; then
-            if [[ -z "$(ls -A "$resolved" 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
-                echo "WARNING:Directory exists but is empty (will be used)"
-                return 1
-            else
-                echo "ERROR:Directory already exists and is not empty"
-                return 2
-            fi
-        else
+        if [[ ! -d "$resolved" ]]; then
             echo "ERROR:Path exists but is not a directory"
             return 2
         fi
+
+        if [[ ! -r "$resolved" || ! -x "$resolved" ]]; then
+            echo "ERROR:Cannot inspect existing directory: $resolved"
+            return 2
+        fi
+
+        if [[ ! -w "$resolved" ]]; then
+            echo "ERROR:Cannot write to existing directory: $resolved"
+            return 2
+        fi
+
+        local first_entry=""
+        first_entry=$(find "$resolved" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null) || {
+            echo "ERROR:Cannot inspect existing directory: $resolved"
+            return 2
+        }
+
+        if [[ -z "$first_entry" ]]; then
+            echo "WARNING:Directory exists but is empty (will be used)"
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        echo "ERROR:Directory already exists and is not empty"
+        return 2
     fi
 
     # Check parent exists
@@ -203,12 +219,21 @@ handle_directory_input() {
             newproj_tty_printf "%s [%s]: " "Directory" "$current_dir"
             # Read from /dev/tty explicitly to avoid stdin conflicts
             # from signal handlers or subshell capture (issue #153)
-            read -r dir < /dev/tty || true
+            if ! read -r dir < /dev/tty; then
+                echo ""
+                return 1
+            fi
             [[ -z "$dir" ]] && dir="$current_dir"
         fi
 
         log_input "directory" "$dir"
 
+        # Handle explicit back command for fallback mode
+        if [[ "$dir" == "<back" ]]; then
+            echo ""
+            return 1
+        fi
+
         # Handle escape/back
         if [[ -z "$dir" ]]; then
             echo ""
@@ -242,7 +267,7 @@ handle_directory_input() {
                 # Warning - confirm with user
                 newproj_tty_printf "%b\n" "${TUI_WARNING}${status#WARNING:}${TUI_NC}"
                 if read_yes_no "Continue anyway?" "y"; then
-                    state_set "project_dir" "$dir"
+                    state_set "project_dir" "$(normalize_path "$dir")"
                     log_validation "directory" "$dir" "PASS" "warning_accepted"
                     valid=true
                 else

@@ -209,7 +209,7 @@ handle_features_input() {
                     # Toggle by numbers
                     for num in $input; do
                         if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le ${#FEATURE_OPTIONS[@]} ]]; then
-                            local opt="${FEATURE_OPTIONS[$((10#num - 1))]}"
+                            local opt="${FEATURE_OPTIONS[$((10#$num - 1))]}"
                             local id="${opt%%:*}"
                             toggle_feature "$id"
                         fi

@@ -92,15 +92,24 @@ handle_project_name_input() {
                 return 1
             }
         else
-            newproj_tty_printf "%s [%s]: " "Project name" "$current_name"
+            newproj_tty_printf "%s [%s]: " "Project Name" "$current_name"
             # Read from /dev/tty explicitly to avoid stdin conflicts
             # from signal handlers or subshell capture (issue #153)
-            read -r name < /dev/tty || true
+            if ! read -r name < /dev/tty; then
+                echo ""
+                return 1
+            fi
             [[ -z "$name" ]] && name="$current_name"
         fi
 
         log_input "project_name" "$name"
 
+        # Handle explicit back command for fallback mode
+        if [[ "$name" == "<back" ]]; then
+            echo ""
+            return 1
+        fi
+
         # Handle escape/back
         if [[ -z "$name" ]]; then
             echo ""

@@ -102,7 +102,7 @@ EOF
 
     if [[ "$(state_get "enable_br")" == "true" && "$beads_initialized" == "true" ]]; then
         echo "  3. Create your first task:"
-        printf "%b\n" "     ${TUI_CYAN}br create \"First feature\" -t feature${TUI_NC}"
+        printf "%b\n" "     ${TUI_CYAN}br create --title=\"First feature\" --type=feature${TUI_NC}"
         echo ""
     elif [[ "$(state_get "enable_br")" == "true" ]]; then
         echo "  3. Finish enabling Beads (optional):"

@@ -194,8 +194,10 @@ handle_tech_stack_input() {
     selected=$(state_get "tech_stack")
 
     if [[ -z "$selected" ]]; then
-        # Try to detect from current directory
-        selected=$(detect_initial_tech_stack)
+        # Only auto-detect from the project directory already collected by the
+        # wizard. Falling back to $PWD misclassifies brand-new projects when the
+        # wizard is launched from inside some unrelated repo.
+        selected=$(detect_initial_tech_stack "$(state_get "project_dir")")
         if [[ -n "$selected" ]]; then
             state_set "detected_tech" "$selected"
         fi
@@ -297,15 +299,15 @@ handle_tech_stack_input() {
                     # Clear selection
                     selected=""
                     ;;
-                'back'|'b')
+                'back'|'b'|'<back')
                     echo ""
                     return 1
                     ;;
                 *)
                     # Toggle by numbers
                     for num in $input; do
                         if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le ${#TECH_STACK_OPTIONS[@]} ]]; then
-                            local opt="${TECH_STACK_OPTIONS[$((10#num - 1))]}"
+                            local opt="${TECH_STACK_OPTIONS[$((10#$num - 1))]}"
                             local key="${opt%%:*}"
                             selected=$(toggle_option "$key" "$selected")
                         fi

@@ -556,8 +556,8 @@ read_selection() {
 
         newproj_tty_printf "%s" "Enter number [1-${#options[@]}]: "
         read -r num < /dev/tty || true
-        if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le ${#options[@]} ]]; then
-            selected="${options[$((10#num - 1))]}"
+        if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le "${#options[@]}" ]]; then
+            selected="${options[$((10#$num - 1))]}"
         fi
     fi
 
@@ -594,9 +594,11 @@ read_checkbox() {
         if [[ "$input" == "all" ]]; then
             selected="${options[*]}"
         else
+            local processed=""
             for num in $input; do
-                if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le ${#options[@]} ]]; then
-                    selected+="${options[$((10#num - 1))]} "
+                if [[ "$num" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$num" -ge 1 ]] && [[ "$num" -le "${#options[@]}" ]]; then
+                    selected+="${options[$((10#$num - 1))]} "
+                    processed+=" $num"
                 fi
             done
         fi

@@ -270,6 +270,22 @@ teardown() {
     [[ "$status" == "WARNING:"* ]]
 }
 
+@test "check_directory_status rejects existing directory without write access" {
+    source_lib "newproj_screens"
+    load_screens
+
+    local locked_dir="$TEST_DIR/locked"
+    mkdir -p "$locked_dir"
+    chmod 500 "$locked_dir"
+
+    local status
+    status=$(check_directory_status "$locked_dir") || true
+
+    chmod 700 "$locked_dir"
+
+    [[ "$status" == "ERROR:Cannot write to existing directory:"* ]]
+}
+
 @test "check_directory_status expands tilde" {
     source_lib "newproj_screens"
     load_screens
